prompt:
The quarterly vendor-spend report (report.tsx, helpers in format.ts — server-rendered via renderToStaticMarkup for the email digest and the dashboard snapshot) is producing wrong output, and finance filed two symptoms against it.

Symptom 1: the numbers drift between renders of the same data. The dashboard snapshot (first render) looks right, but the email digest — which renders the exact same props again immediately afterwards — disagrees with it: the over-budget count drops, and the variance list pairs vendors with the wrong budget lines (a vendor that was "over by $300" on the first paint comes back "under by $7,900" on the second). A third render agrees with the second, not the first. Nothing external changes between renders; it's the same arrays from the planning-sheet importer both times.

Symptom 2: vendor names with special characters are mangled everywhere the name shows up. "O'Brien & Co" reaches the reader as the literal text "O&#39;Brien &amp; Co" — you can see the entity source with your own eyes in the rendered page. Oddly, the footnote line is fine: its ampersand and *emphasis* markup come out exactly right, so it doesn't look like an encoding problem in the toolchain.

Both report.tsx and format.ts are in the workspace. Find what's actually wrong and fix it — keep the surgery minimal (same exports, same class names, same element structure; the tests pin the markup). Done means `node run_tests.mjs` exits green. That harness is read-only: it bundles report.tsx + format.ts with the React 19.2.7 vendored under ./vendor/node_modules (esbuild binary on PATH, fully offline — no npm, no network) and asserts on the renderToStaticMarkup output with attribute order/case normalized. Don't touch run_tests.mjs or vendor/.
notes: react 19.2.7 + react-dom 19.2.7 + scheduler 0.27.0 (production cjs, complete packages minus READMEs) vendored in files/vendor/node_modules; harness bundles via esbuild on PATH, offline; .bundle_out/ is created and removed by the harness. Debug seed with exactly two planted causes, never named in the prompt: (1) report.tsx TopVendorsTable calls vendors.sort() in place on the props array, so the first render silently reorders the shared vendors array while budgets keeps importer order — on the second render SummaryCards/VarianceList index-align the wrong pairs (totals/variance drift, renders 2+ stable-wrong); fix ranks a copy ([...vendors].sort(...)). (2) format.ts vendorLabel() pre-escapes with escapeHtml() and callers use the result as a JSX text child, so React escapes it again — double-escaped entities; fix returns name.trim() and lets React do the single escape. Leave-one-out holds: cause 1 alone fails the re-render/mutation tests, cause 2 alone fails the escaping/markup tests. The footnote path (escapeHtml -> emphasize -> dangerouslySetInnerHTML) is intentionally correct and pinned by a test so fixers don't 'repair' it.
